answer:
**The *Viṣṇu Smṛti* (slug: `unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8`), Chapter 1, opens precisely with this narrative.**

The chapter begins with the Earth submerged in the cosmic waters at the end of Brahmā's night. Viṣṇu, in the form of a Boar (Varāha), rescues her: "Took on the auspicious form of the Boar... And lifted up the Earth."[^1] His form is described in sacrificial terms: "whose tusks were the sacrificial post... whose tongue was the sacrificial fire."[^2]

After recreating the world and vanishing—"Thus the blessed Boar... Went to a destination then unknown to the worlds"[^3]—the Earth-goddess (Vasudhā/Dhara/Prithvi) ponders her support: "What will be my support now?" She resolves: "I will go and ask Kashyapa."[^4]

Taking the form of a beautiful woman—"her lips auspicious and red like the Bandhujīva flower"[^5]—she approaches the sage. Kashyapa directs her: his abode "is in the Ocean of Milk... Proceed[ed] to the Ocean of Milk to see Keśava (Vishnu)."[^6]

There, she finds the reclining god ("resting upon the couch of Śeṣa") and asks: "what will be my support?"[^7] Viṣṇu seats her "on this excellent golden throne" to expound the law (*dharma*) of castes (*varṇas*) and orders (*āśramas*): "Be seated... hear now the laws."[^8]

[^1]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.2, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-bb3692](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-bb3692).

[^2]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.3, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-979e7a](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-979e7a).

[^3]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.18, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-d6fb26](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-d6fb26).

[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.19–20, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-7b2100](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-7b2100).

[^5]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.22, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-7965d8](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-7965d8).

[^6]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.32–33, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-2b042c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-2b042c).

[^7]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.46, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-5eec19](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-5eec19).

[^8]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.64, [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-398224](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-398224).

The full opening sequence (verses 1–18, on the Boar incarnation and vanishing) is available here: [/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-88086c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-88086c). The entire chapter provides the context leading to the exposition of *dharma*.
